title: 10 best herbal capsules for colds

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with colds because they contain a concentrated extract of the leaves of Adiantum capillus-veneris, which has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to alleviate respiratory issues. The capsule's active compounds, such as flavonoids and saponins, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce congestion, soothe sore throats, and combat viral infections that cause colds. By supporting the body's natural defenses, maidenhair tree capsules can provide relief from cold symptoms and promote a faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba capsules contain powerful antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can exacerbate cold symptoms such as congestion and fatigue.

Inflammation-reducing flavonoids: The capsules contain flavonoids, including quercetin, kaempferol, and isorhapontigenin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages and sinuses, providing relief from cold symptoms.

Immunomodulatory effects: Ginkgo biloba capsules have been shown to modulate the immune system, enhancing its natural defenses against infection and reducing the severity of cold symptoms by boosting antibody production and activating natural killer cells.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with colds, promoting overall well-being and alleviating symptoms.

Terpenoids (specifically Bilobalide and Ginkgolides): These terpenoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate cold symptoms such as congestion and cough by reducing inflammation in the airways.

Glycosides (specifically Quercetin and Isorhapontigenin): These compounds have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help combat the oxidative stress and inflammation associated with colds, thus reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: These are the primary source of Ginkgo biloba extract, which is used to treat various health conditions, including colds.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to alleviate cold symptoms.
Barks: The bark of the Ginkgo biloba tree is sometimes used to make supplements, containing compounds that may help to reduce congestion and promote a healthy respiratory system during colds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1.5 to 2 pounds of ginkgo biloba leaves and flowers in the fall when they are fully ripe.
Dry the ginkgo biloba leaves and flowers in a warm dry place for 1 to 2 weeks.
Grind 500 grams of dried ginkgo biloba into a fine powder using a grinder or mortar.
Mix 250 grams of the powdered ginkgo biloba with 50 grams of a carrier powder like rice flour to create a blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 0.5 to 1 gram of the ginkgo biloba powder blend using a capsule filling machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower capsules helps with colds because they contain the active compound echinacea, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties. When taken at the onset of a cold, these capsules can help reduce the severity and duration of symptoms by boosting the immune system's response to the invading virus or bacteria. Additionally, purple coneflower's antioxidant properties can also help combat oxidative stress caused by the infection, promoting overall recovery and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antiviral compounds: The capsules contain antiviral compounds such as cinnamic acid, chicoric acid, and polyphenols, which help to inhibit the replication of viruses responsible for common colds.

Immune system support: Purple Coneflower contains bioactive compounds that have immunomodulatory effects, stimulating the immune response to fight off infections and reducing the severity of cold symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation in the body and alleviate symptoms associated with common colds, such as congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help to boost the immune system by increasing the production of white blood cells, which fight off infections that cause colds.
Caffeic acid: As an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory, caffeic acid helps to reduce the severity and duration of cold symptoms by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Alkylphenol glycosides: These compounds have been shown to exhibit immunomodulatory effects, which help to regulate the immune response and reduce the risk of developing colds.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, making them a primary source of Echinacea's medicinal properties.
Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and other compounds that contribute to the plant's immune-boosting and anti-inflammatory effects.
Flowers: They contain alkylamides and other compounds that help to stimulate the immune system and reduce the severity of cold and flu symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of dried echinacea purpurea flowers and roots from a clean and dry workspace.
Crush 2 tablespoons of the harvested plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the powder with 1/2 teaspoon of gelatin and 1/2 teaspoon of water to form a paste.
Use a capsule machine to fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared paste, holding the machine steady.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: King of Bitters capsules helps with colds because they stimulate the immune system, boosting its natural defense against invading pathogens. The herbs in the formula, such as Gentian and Angelica, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce congestion and soothe a sore throat. Additionally, the bitter compounds activate digestive enzymes, which can aid in the elimination of toxins and promote overall wellness. This holistic approach helps to alleviate cold symptoms and supports the body's natural healing process.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: The capsules' ingredients, such as Andrographis paniculata and Echinacea spp., have been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses that cause colds, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbs like Guduchi (Tinospora cordifolia) and Turmeric (Curcuma longa) in the capsules have anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce congestion, sinus pressure, and other symptoms associated with colds.

Immune system support: The capsules' blend of herbs, including Ginseng (Panax spp.) and Licorice root (Glycyrrhiza glabra), may help stimulate the immune system to fight off infections more effectively, reducing the risk of complications from colds.
remedy_constituents: Andrographolide: This diterpenoid lactone helps with colds by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which can reduce the severity and duration of cold symptoms.

Neochlorogenic acid (also known as 3-O-caffeoylquinic acid): This phenolic compound has antioxidant properties that help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by cold viruses, potentially reducing the risk of complications.
Andrographiside: This diterpenoid lactone has been shown to have antiviral properties, which can help inhibit the replication of cold viruses and reduce the severity of cold symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of andrographolide, a key compound responsible for the herb's medicinal properties.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of Andrographis paniculata contain a high amount of andrographolide, making them a popular alternative to leaves for producing capsules.
Stems: The stems of the plant are also used, although to a lesser extent, as they still contain significant amounts of andrographolide, contributing to their medicinal value.
remedy_recipe: Dry the andrographis paniculata leaves for 2 hours at 50 degrees celsius in a dehydrator to preserve their properties.
Grind 1:8 ratio of dried andrographis paniculata leaves to an inert filler like tapioca powder into a fine powder.
Weigh out 500 milligrams of the powdered andrographis paniculata mixture and add it to a capsule.
Fill 100 capsules with the powdered andrographis paniculata mixture within 30 minutes of preparation to maintain potency.
Label the capsules with the name andrographis paniculata and the dosage of 500 milligrams per capsule.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm capsules helps with colds because of its natural ability to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the throat, nose, and chest. The mucilage extract in slippery elm capsules forms a protective barrier that shields against inflammation, reduces congestion, and promotes effortless mucus clearance. This relief from discomfort allows the body to focus on fighting off the infection, reducing the severity of symptoms and promoting faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Slippery elm's mucilages (gel-like substances) help reduce inflammation in the throat and respiratory tract, providing relief from soreness and discomfort.

Mucilage formation: When combined with water, slippery elm's mucilages form a protective coating that soothes and protects the mucous membranes in the throat and respiratory tract, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by cold symptoms.

Immune system support: Slippery elm contains antioxidants and other bioactive compounds that may help boost the immune system, supporting the body's natural defenses against infection and reducing the severity of cold symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce the severity of cold symptoms such as congestion and sore throats.
Flavonoids (Quercetin): As an antiviral and anti-inflammatory agent, Quercetin may help combat viral infections and alleviate symptoms such as runny nose and cough.
Phenolic acids (Gallic acid): Gallic acid exhibits antimicrobial properties, which may help prevent the growth of pathogens that cause colds and other respiratory infections.
remedy_parts: Barks: The bark of Ulmus rubra is used due to its expectorant properties, helping to relieve congestion and coughs associated with colds.
Leaves: The leaves of Ulmus rubra are used for their antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to combat cold and flu viruses.
Roots: The roots of Ulmus rubra are used for their antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce the severity of cold and flu symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 40 grams of dried ulmus rubra bark from a sustainable source with a 3-year-old tree.
Grind the bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powder with 20 grams of gelatin and 10 grams of glycerin in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the powder mixture using a powder scoop.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with colds because they contain mucilage, a natural thickening agent that soothes and protects irritated respiratory tissues. When taken as a supplement, marshmallow root helps to lubricate the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with colds. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties can help to alleviate congestion and coughing, making it easier to breathe and rest while your body fights off the infection.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Marshmallow root contains a thick, protective gel-like substance called mucilage that helps soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the throat and respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with colds.

Antioxidant properties: Marshmallow capsules have antioxidant properties that help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation caused by the common cold virus, reducing tissue damage and promoting a faster recovery.

Expectorant effects: The saponins present in marshmallow root have expectorant properties, which means they help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and breathe more easily when experiencing congestion and chest tightness due to a cold.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages help soothe and calm the throat and respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with colds.
Glucosides (such as ursolic acid): Glucosides have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce congestion and ease respiratory issues, making it easier to breathe while dealing with a cold.
Flavonoids (such as quercetin): Flavonoids possess antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat the underlying viral infection and alleviate symptoms associated with colds.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilages that help soothe sore throats and calm coughs.
Leaves: Contain compounds that can help reduce inflammation and combat cold-related infections.
Buds: Possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can aid in alleviating cold symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest althaea officinalis roots and clean them thoroughly to remove any debris or dirt.
Dry the cleaned althaea officinalis roots in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried althaea officinalis roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 1000 mg of the ground althaea officinalis powder with 500 mg of cellulose and 100 mg of magnesium stearate.
Fill a capsule machine with the powder mixture and seal the capsules according to manufacturer's instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder capsules helps with colds because they contain extracts of elderberry, a natural antiviral compound that targets the virus and prevents its replication. The anthocyanins and flavonoids present in elderberry have potent anti-inflammatory properties, reducing congestion and alleviating symptoms such as sore throat, cough, and runny nose. Additionally, elder capsules support immune function by stimulating the production of cytokines, which help to fight off infection and promote recovery from a cold. By supplementing with elder capsules, individuals can naturally boost their immune system and recover faster from a cold.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Elderberry's antiviral compounds, such as anthocyanins and flavonoids, help to inhibit the growth and replication of viruses like the flu, reducing the severity and duration of cold symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Elderberry's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier to breathe and alleviating cold symptoms.
Mucus-thinning properties: Elderberry's mucilages and glycosides can help to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion and coughing.
remedy_constituents: Vitamin C: Helps to boost the immune system and reduce the severity of cold symptoms by enhancing the production of white blood cells.
Flavonoids (including quercetin and kaempferol): Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can help to reduce the severity of cold symptoms and combat viral infections.
Sambunigrin (a glycoside): Demonstrates immunomodulatory and antiviral activities that can help to enhance the immune response and protect against viral infections, such as the common cold.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain bioflavonoids and antioxidants that help to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation associated with colds.
Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe a sore throat and reduce the severity of cold symptoms.
Fruits (or more specifically, Berries): They are rich in vitamin C and antioxidants that can help to boost the immune system and reduce the severity of cold symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of fresh sambucus nigra flowers and leaves on a sunny morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in a warm place at 30-40 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind 1 tablespoon of dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 500 mg of the powdered sambucus nigra with 500 mg of a natural capsule filler to create a uniform blend.
Fill 100-200 vegetarian capsules with the sambucus nigra powder blend and seal them securely by hand.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic capsules helps with colds because they contain potent compounds that have natural antiviral and antibacterial properties. The sulfur-rich extracts in these capsules work to boost the immune system, targeting the viruses and bacteria that cause colds and helping to alleviate symptoms such as congestion, coughing, and sore throats. Additionally, garlic's antioxidant properties help to reduce inflammation and promote overall health, making it a natural and effective way to combat colds.
remedy_properties: Vitamin C: Garlic contains vitamin C, which is essential for boosting the immune system to fight off infections like the common cold, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.

Allicin: Allicin, a sulfur compound found in garlic, has antibacterial, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties that can help combat viral and bacterial infections, alleviate congestion, and reduce coughing.

Quercetin: Quercetin, a flavonoid found in garlic, is a natural antihistamine that can help reduce inflammation and congestion associated with colds, making it easier to breathe and relieving sinus pressure.
remedy_constituents: Allicin: Allicin is a sulfur compound that helps break down mucus and reduce congestion, making it easier to breathe and relieving cold symptoms.
Quercetin: Quercetin is a flavonoid antioxidant that helps reduce inflammation and fight off viral infections, such as the common cold, by inhibiting the replication of viruses.
Diallyl disulfide: Diallyl disulfide is a sulfur compound that has antimicrobial properties, helping to kill bacteria and viruses that cause colds, and reducing the severity of symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of garlic contain compounds with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate cold symptoms.
Buds: Garlic buds are rich in antioxidants and have been traditionally used to boost the immune system and fight off infections associated with colds.
Roots: The roots of garlic contain compounds that have antiviral properties, which help prevent the replication of viruses that cause colds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 200g of fresh or dried allium sativum roots with a focus on quality and consistency.
Clean the harvested roots thoroughly under running water to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the cleaned roots in a dehydrator at 50°c for 24 hours or until they reach 8% moisture content.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Fill vegetarian capsules with 250mg of the powdered root, approximately 100 capsules can be filled.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with colds because they contain natural compounds like gingerols and shogaols that have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help to reduce congestion and soothe a sore throat, making it easier to breathe and talk. Additionally, ginger has natural decongestant and expectorant properties that can help to loosen and clear mucus from the chest and lungs, allowing for faster recovery from a cold.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, can help reduce inflammation in the throat and chest, which can ease congestion and coughing associated with colds.
Expectorant properties: Ginger's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion and coughing.

Antioxidant activity: Ginger's antioxidant properties can help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate cold symptoms, such as fatigue and fever, by supporting the body's natural defense mechanisms.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols, particularly 6-gingerol and 8-gingerol, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce congestion and alleviate cold symptoms.
Shogaols: Shogaols, particularly 6-shogaol, exhibit antimicrobial activity, which helps combat viral and bacterial infections that cause colds, and also reduce inflammation.
Terpenes (specifically, Zingiberene): Zingiberene, a sesquiterpene, has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that help relieve congestion, cough, and sore throat associated with colds.
remedy_parts: Roots: Although roots are a part of the rhizome, some sources specifically mention roots being used; however, rhizomes are more commonly referenced.
Buds: Ginger buds are sometimes used for their concentrated flavor and medicinal properties; however, rhizomes are the more commonly referenced part.
remedy_recipe: Harvest a sufficient quantity of dried zingiber officinale root in a clean environment with sterile tools to prevent contamination.
Grind 1000 mg of the harvested root into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder to ensure uniformity.
Mix 500 mg of the powder with 500 mg of a natural filler material in a stainless steel container.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with the combined powder mixture using a capsule filling machine at a rate of 200 capsules per hour.
Label and store the filled capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years to preserve potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with colds because they contain menthol, a natural decongestant that can ease sinus pressure and relieve congestion. The cooling properties of peppermint also help to reduce inflammation in the throat and nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and swallow. Additionally, peppermint has antimicrobial properties that can help combat the underlying infection, reducing the severity and duration of cold symptoms such as coughing, sneezing, and runny nose.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol's analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects help relieve congestion, coughing, and sore throat discomfort associated with colds.
Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol's decongestant properties help break down mucus, making it easier to expel, which can alleviate sinus pressure and congestion caused by colds.

Volatile Oils: The volatile oils present in peppermint, such as menthone and limonene, have natural antibacterial properties that help combat the underlying infection causing the common cold, reducing its severity and duration.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Acts as a natural decongestant, helping to relieve nasal congestion and cough associated with colds by thinning mucus and promoting its expulsion.
Rosmarinic acid (phenolic compound): Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the severity of cold symptoms such as sore throat, cough, and runny nose.
Caryophyllene (terpene): Possesses expectorant properties, aiding in the relief of cough and congestion by loosening and removing mucus from the airways.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Mentha x piperita are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which provide analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties to help alleviate cold symptoms.
Stems: The stems of Mentha x piperita are also used due to their high menthol content, which helps to ease congestion and relieve coughs associated with colds.
Buds: The buds of Mentha x piperita contain high amounts of menthol and menthone, making them a popular choice for cold relief capsules, as they help to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and stems in the early morning when the essential oil is most concentrated.
Dry the harvested mentha x piperita in a single layer at room temperature for 2-3 days to reduce moisture content.
Crush 2 teaspoons of dried mentha x piperita into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 seconds.
Mix the powder with 2 tablespoons of gelatin and 2 tablespoons of water in a saucepan and heat the mixture over low heat for 5 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 1/2 teaspoon of the cooled mentha x piperita mixture and seal the capsules immediately to prevent moisture absorption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with colds because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that exhibits anti-inflammatory properties. When taken as a supplement, it can help soothe a sore throat and reduce congestion by thinning mucus, making it easier to expel. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has been shown to boost the immune system's response to infection, helping the body fight off the cold virus more effectively. As a natural remedy, licorice capsules offer a gentle yet effective way to alleviate cold symptoms and support overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice root has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling in the throat, nasal passages, and sinuses, relieving congestion and discomfort associated with colds.

Expectorant properties: Herbal licorice contains expectorants like glycyrrhizin and isoflavones, which can help loosen and clear out mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and breathe more easily.

Antimicrobial properties: Licorice root has antimicrobial compounds that can help combat bacterial and viral infections, reducing the severity of cold symptoms like sore throats, coughs, and congestion.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the severity of cold symptoms such as congestion and coughing.
Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin, has expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and cough up phlegm.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly isoflavones, in Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by cold viruses, supporting the immune system and reducing the severity of cold symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps alleviate congestion and coughs associated with colds.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and other compounds that contribute to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe cold-related symptoms.
Roots: They also contain other compounds like licoricidin and licorisoflavan A, which have antimicrobial properties and can help combat cold-causing pathogens.
remedy_recipe: Collect 100g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root and sift it through a 20-mesh sieve to remove lumps and impurities.
Measure out 5g of the sifted root and blend it into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powdered root with 10g of vegetable cellulose powder and 5g of silicon dioxide in a clean bowl.
Use a capsule filling machine to fill 100 empty vegetable capsules with the powder mixture in 5 minutes.
Label and date the capsules and store them in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for long-term preservation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with colds because they contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that targets the root cause of congestion, sinus pressure, and coughing. Curcumin's potent antioxidant properties also help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and alleviating discomfort. Additionally, turmeric has natural expectorant properties that help loosen mucus, allowing for easy drainage and relief from a stuffy nose and throat.
remedy_properties: Curcumin's Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Reduces inflammation and swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, helping to alleviate congestion and sinus pressure.
Antioxidant Activity: Neutralizes free radicals and helps protect against oxidative stress caused by viral infections, which can exacerbate cold symptoms like coughing and sneezing.
Immunomodulatory Effects: Boosts immune function by modulating the activity of immune cells, helping to fight off invading pathogens and reduce the severity and duration of cold symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate congestion and soothe respiratory issues associated with colds.

Roots: Contain a variety of bioactive compounds, including volatile oils and sesquiterpenes, that possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, making them effective in treating cold symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh curcuma longa roots from a trusted source and wash them thoroughly under running water.
Dry the washed curcuma longa roots in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator for 3-5 hours.
Grind the dried curcuma longa roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500mg of the ground curcuma longa powder with 200mg of gelatin capsules and fill into empty vegan capsules.
Store the prepared herbal capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon capsules helps with colds because of its natural antimicrobial properties, which help to combat the viral and bacterial infections that cause respiratory issues. The cinnamaldehyde present in these capsules has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria such as E. coli and Staphylococcus aureus, reducing inflammation and congestion caused by a cold. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon's warm and comforting properties can help to soothe a sore throat and relieve coughing, providing relief from symptoms associated with a cold.
remedy_properties: Cinnamaldehyde: This compound has natural antimicrobial properties that help combat cold-causing viruses and bacteria, reducing the severity of symptoms.
Cinnamon oil: The oil extracted from Ceylon cinnamon has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce congestion and alleviate sinus pressure associated with colds.
Flavonoids: These compounds have antioxidant properties that can help boost the immune system, reducing the risk of developing a cold in the first place or shortening its duration once contracted.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the underground stems that contain high levels of cinnamaldehyde, a natural compound with anti-inflammatory properties that can help relieve congestion and soothe sore throats.
Barks: The barks of the Ceylon cinnamon plant have been traditionally used to treat colds and flu due to their antimicrobial and antiviral properties, which can help reduce the severity of symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cinnamomum verum bark in the morning after dew has evaporated, selecting pieces free of mold and insects.
Dry the harvested cinnamomum verum bark in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 24 hours.
Grind 20 grams of dried cinnamomum verum bark into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground cinnamomum verum bark powder with 100 grams of gelatin capsules, filling each capsule to approximately 10% capacity.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or freeze for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum capsules helps with colds because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The capsules contain a concentrated extract of Eucalyptus globulus, which is known for its decongestant and expectorant effects. When taken as a supplement, the capsules can help to reduce nasal congestion and sinus pressure, making it easier to breathe and sleep. Additionally, the antioxidants present in the blue gum may also help to boost the immune system, reducing the severity and duration of cold symptoms.
remedy_properties: Decongestant property: Tasmanian Blue Gum capsules contain compounds like eucalyptol, which helps to relieve congestion and reduce nasal secretions, making it easier to breathe.

Anti-inflammatory property: The capsules' anti-inflammatory properties may help to alleviate sinus pressure, headaches, and sore throats associated with colds, by reducing swelling and inflammation in the mucous membranes.
Expectorant property: Tasmanian Blue Gum's expectorant properties can help loosen and expel mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and relieve congestion in the chest.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are rich in bioactive compounds, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective against common cold symptoms.

Barks: The bark of the Tasmanian Blue Gum contains phenolic acids and flavonoids that have been found to possess antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities, helping to alleviate cold-related congestion and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one cup of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves and store them in an airtight container.
Boil two cups of water and add one teaspoon of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle and let it cool.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with 250 milligrams of the cooled liquid extract and seal them tightly.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to two weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme capsules helps with colds because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which work to combat the viruses that cause upper respiratory tract infections. The essential oils present in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of various bacteria, fungi, and viruses, including those responsible for common cold symptoms like congestion and coughing. By supplementing with herbal thyme capsules, individuals can boost their immune system and potentially reduce the severity and duration of colds.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Thyme's expectorant property helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion.

Antibacterial property: Thyme's antibacterial property helps to combat bacterial infections that can exacerbate cold symptoms, such as sinusitis and bronchitis, by inhibiting the growth of bacteria like Streptococcus pneumoniae.
Anti-inflammatory property: Thyme's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce inflammation in the airways and nasal passages, which can alleviate symptoms like coughing, sneezing, and runny nose.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their antibacterial and antiviral properties, thyme leaves help combat cold-causing pathogens and reduce symptoms such as coughing and congestion.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and terpenes, thyme flowers possess anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that aid in relieving chest congestion and sore throats associated with the common cold.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves from a pesticide-free garden or store-dried ones in an airtight container.
Dry the harvested thymus vulgaris leaves in a warm oven at 60°c for 24 hours to preserve their potency.
Grind 30 grams of dried thymus vulgaris leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the thymus vulgaris powder and seal them tightly to maintain freshness.
Store the herbal thymus vulgaris capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hyssop capsules helps with colds because of its natural antimicrobial properties, which effectively combat the viral and bacterial infections that cause respiratory issues. The herb's active compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and camphor, work to reduce congestion, soothe a sore throat, and ease coughing. Additionally, hyssop's anti-inflammatory properties help alleviate sinus pressure and promote overall respiratory health. By incorporating herbal hyssop capsules into your wellness routine, you can support your body's natural defense against colds and flu, helping to reduce the duration and severity of symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Hyssop's antiviral compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help to combat the viral pathogens responsible for common colds, reducing their replication and spread.
Expectorant properties: Hyssop's natural expectorants, like mucilage and volatile oils, loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion and coughing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Hyssop's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating symptoms like sore throats and sinus pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hyssop roots have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma, making them an effective choice for soothing coughs and congestion in medicinal capsules.
Leaves: The leaves of the hyssop plant contain essential oils that have antibacterial properties, helping to combat cold-causing bacteria and reduce the severity of symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hyssopus officinalis leaves and stems in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 30°c for 24 hours.
Grind 100g of dried hyssopus officinalis into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 5g of ground hyssopus officinalis with 95g of tapioca starch and capsule filler to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the blend mixture and seal according to manufacturer instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with colds because of its natural antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb calends, also known as pot marigold, contains flavonoids and terpenoids that have been shown to inhibit the growth of viruses, reducing the severity and duration of a cold. Additionally, the herb's anti-inflammatory compounds help to ease congestion, reduce fever, and soothe a sore throat, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving the symptoms of a cold.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Pot marigold contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds like lutein, zeaxanthin, and flavonoids, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, alleviating symptoms of colds such as congestion, coughing, and sore throats.

Expectorant and Mucolytic Properties: The capsules contain saponins, which help break down mucus and thin it out, making it easier to expel from the respiratory tract, reducing congestion and relieving coughs and sneezing.

Immunomodulatory Properties: Pot marigold has been traditionally used to enhance immunity and fight off infections, including viral and bacterial infections like the common cold. The immunomodulatory compounds in the capsules help stimulate the immune system, allowing it to respond more effectively to invading pathogens.
remedy_parts: Roots: Potent in anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective against respiratory issues and congestion caused by colds.
Seeds: Rich in antioxidants and fatty acids, which help to reduce inflammation and soothe sore throats.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers, ensuring they are free of pesticides and other contaminants for use.
Dry the calendula flowers in a warm air circulation oven at 150°f for 1-2 hours until brittle and crumbly.
Grind the dried calendula into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle to release essential oils.
Mix 500mg of calendula powder with 250mg of silica gel powder in a small bowl for optimal capsule fill.
Fill empty vegan capsules with the mixture and seal immediately to preserve potency and freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock capsules helps with colds because of its unique ability to stimulate the lymphatic system, which plays a crucial role in flushing out toxins and excess mucus from the body. The plant-based compounds present in burdock roots have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce congestion and soothe a sore throat. Additionally, burdock's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out phlegm, making it easier to expel, which can provide quick relief from cold symptoms such as coughing and sneezing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Burdock root has anti-inflammatory compounds like polyphenols and flavonoids that can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating congestion and discomfort associated with colds.

Immune system support: The bioactive compounds in burdock, such as taraxasteryl acetate, have immunomodulatory effects, which means they can enhance the body's natural defense against infections like the common cold by stimulating immune cell activity.

Antioxidant and antimicrobial: Burdock root contains a range of antioxidants, including vitamin C and beta-carotene, that help protect the body from oxidative stress and microbial infections, while its antimicrobial properties may also help combat bacterial and viral invaders responsible for colds.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in inulin, a prebiotic fiber that helps boost immunity and reduce inflammation associated with cold symptoms.
Seeds: Containing polyphenols and flavonoids that exhibit antiviral and antibacterial properties, helping to combat the underlying causes of colds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried arctium lapa root.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm dark place for two weeks.
Grind 30g of dried arctium lapa root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 10% glycerin with 90% distilled water to create a solvent solution.
Fill #0 capsules with 500mg of the powdered root mixture and seal.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with colds because they contain natural diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to alleviate congestion and sinus pressure. The plant's leaves and flowers are rich in vitamins A, C, and K, which boost the immune system and support overall health. Additionally, dandelion's antibacterial and antiviral compounds work to combat infection-causing pathogens, helping to shorten the duration and severity of cold symptoms. By incorporating dandelion capsules into your daily routine, you can experience natural relief from cold and flu symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion contains compounds like taraxasterol, which has been shown to reduce inflammation in the body, helping to alleviate symptoms such as sore throats and congestion.
Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's antioxidant content helps protect the body from free radicals that can exacerbate cold symptoms like fatigue and weakness, supporting overall immune function.
Immunomodulatory properties: Dandelion's immunomodulatory effects help regulate the immune system's response to infection, potentially reducing the severity and duration of a cold.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in relieving congestion and reducing cough severity.
Seeds: Containing oil with anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe a sore throat and ease respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion leaves and flowers on a sunny morning for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm oven at 150°f for 6-8 hours to preserve quality.
Combine dried dandelion leaves and flowers with gelatin capsules and fill 100-count capsules.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.
Take 3-4 capsules twice daily for 7-10 days to alleviate cold symptoms and boost immunity.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow capsules helps with colds because they contain a concentrated extract of the herb yarrow, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce congestion and alleviate symptoms such as runny nose, sore throat, and cough. The flavonoids present in yarrow also have antioxidant properties, which can help to boost the immune system and combat the underlying viral infection causing the cold. Additionally, yarrow's expectorant properties can help to loosen mucus and promote its elimination from the body, providing relief from chest congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Yarrow capsules contain compounds that help to combat bacteria, viruses, and fungi, which can contribute to the severity of cold symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory effects can help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, sinuses, and throat, making it easier to breathe and alleviating discomfort.
Expectorant properties: The mucilage present in yarrow capsules helps to thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel from the body and relieving coughing and congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and polyphenols, which help to alleviate cold symptoms such as congestion and fever.
Flowers: Containing apigenin, a natural antihistamine that can help to relieve allergies and sinus pressure associated with colds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried achillea millefolium flowers in sufficient quantity for the desired dosage.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or food processor.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the powdered herb with 2 tablespoons of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Allow the mixture to infuse for 2-3 hours before straining through cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250mg of the infused herbal oil and store in an airtight container.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for colds are a natural remedy designed to alleviate symptoms associated with upper respiratory infections. These capsules contain potent herbs, such as echinacea, goldenseal, and slippery elm, which work together to boost the immune system, reduce congestion, and soothe sore throats. For example, echinacea has been shown to increase the production of white blood cells, while goldenseal's antibacterial properties help combat infection. By taking these herbal capsules regularly, individuals can experience reduced severity and duration of cold symptoms, allowing them to get back to their daily lives with improved energy and vitality.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to colds that are treated with herbal capsules are sinusitis, bronchitis, and flu-like symptoms. Herbal capsules containing ingredients like Andrographis paniculata, Echinacea purpurea, and Ginkgo biloba help alleviate congestion, coughing, and fever. They may also reduce inflammation and boost the immune system to fight off infections. Additionally, herbal capsules can be used to treat allergies, asthma, and other respiratory issues that share similarities with colds.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with colds is Echinacea and Goldenseal. Echinacea enhances the immune system, reducing the severity and duration of cold symptoms. Goldenseal has antimicrobial properties, which help combat bacterial and viral infections. Some people also add Zambu or Yarrow to reduce inflammation and soothe a sore throat. However, consult a healthcare professional before taking any herbal supplements, as their interactions with medications or underlying health conditions can be unpredictable.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Arzneimittel-Forschung, the study aimed to confirm the effectiveness of Echinaceae purpureae herba (Echinacin, EC31J0) in relieving common cold symptoms. The study used a randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled clinical trial, recruiting a total of 80 adult male or female patients with first signs of a cold. The results found that the median time of illness was 6.0 days in the verum group, who took Echinacin, compared to 9.0 days in the placebo group, indicating that Echinacin can help alleviate symptoms more rapidly and is a good option for managing colds.
